description_long: This object consists of a group of four medicine bottles of varying sizes and colors, typically found in pharmacies or healthcare settings. The largest bottle is white with a wide cylindrical body and a screw cap, featuring a pale orange label with text. A medium-sized orange bottle with a white cap and label stands in front, commonly used for prescription tablets. Two smaller bottles, one white and one dark brown, are also present, each with white caps and printed labels. The arrangement suggests a collection of over-the-counter or prescription medications, with each bottle designed to securely contain tablets or capsules. The labels display product names and information, though some text is partially obscured or illegible. The bottles are made of plastic, with smooth, hard surfaces and typical pharmaceutical packaging features.
description: A group of four medicine bottles in white, orange, and brown, each with printed labels and screw caps, commonly used for tablets or capsules.
description_view_0: Front view showing all four bottles, with labels and caps visible; the orange and brown bottles are most prominent.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the white and orange bottles, with the brown bottle mostly hidden behind.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the backs of the largest white bottle and the smaller white and orange bottles; labels are not visible.
description_view_3: Angled view focusing on the largest white bottle and the brown bottle in front, with the orange bottle partially visible.
